summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Tactics to combat fraud and protect your business reputation
articles.description: Businesses can adopt tools like tokenization to combat fraud and protect their reputations.

articles.title: Mastercard: Revenue growth, emissions reduction can coincide
articles.description: The card network reduced its corporate carbon footprint again last year, even as revenue grew, according to an Earth Day blog post this week.

articles.title: Why credit card rate caps hurt consumers, small businesses
articles.description: “Affordability is not just about the cost of credit," a former Treasury official writes. "It is about whether credit is available at all.”

articles.title: What drives real-time payment growth
articles.description: Corporations adopt faster digital payments based on their priorities and the rise of digital assets, an HSBC payments executive said.

articles.title: Amex CEO gushes over next gen
articles.description: The card company’s CEO, Steve Squeri, believes the younger cohort of Amex card holders are suited to withstand economic storms.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard face new suit over card fees
articles.description: Three plaintiffs say a 2019 settlement in card fee litigation should not give the card networks years of legal immunity for antitrust claims.

articles.title: Repay attracts $1B takeover offer
articles.description: The payments processing services company is the target of an unsolicited offer from an investor, just as it’s trying to acquire another company.

articles.title: ACH Network starts 2026 with surge
articles.description: The payments network saw growth across healthcare, peer-to-peer, online and direct deposits in the first quarter, pushing the volume value past $24 trillion.
